qa_pairs.question: How can I resolve a module loading error for 'quantumespresso/6.1' if a prerequisite is missing?
qa_pairs.answer: To resolve a module loading error for 'quantumespresso/6.1' when a prerequisite is missing, you should add the line `module load nixpkgs/16.09 intel/2016.4 openmpi/2.1.1` to your job script before loading `quantumespresso/6.1`.

qa_pairs.question: Do Slurm jobs automatically inherit environment variables from the shell where they were submitted?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, by default, a job will inherit the environment variables of the shell from which it was submitted.

qa_pairs.question: How can changes to environment variables made by the `module` command affect a job's ability to load modules?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes made by the `module` command, which is used to make various software packages available, will propagate to any job submitted from the shell and could affect the job's ability to load modules if there are missing prerequisites.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended practice for ensuring a consistent module loading state for each Slurm job submission?
qa_pairs.answer: It is best to include the line `module purge` in your job script before loading all the required modules to ensure a consistent state for each job submission and avoid changes made in your shell affecting your jobs.

qa_pairs.question: How can I prevent a Slurm job from inheriting environment settings from the submitting shell?
qa_pairs.answer: If you wish to suppress the inheritance of environment settings from the submitting shell, use the `--export=none` directive when submitting jobs.

qa_pairs.question: Why might a submitted Slurm job appear to hang and produce no output for an extended period?
qa_pairs.answer: A common reason a submitted job might write no output to the log file for an extended period, appearing to hang, is the aggressive output buffering performed by the Slurm scheduler, which aggregates many output lines before flushing them to the log file.

qa_pairs.question: When is the output file typically written for a Slurm job, and what happens if a job is cancelled or runs out of time?
qa_pairs.answer: Often the output file will only be written after the job completes; and if the job is cancelled (or runs out of time), part of the output may be lost.

qa_pairs.question: What is a good way to monitor the progress of a running Slurm job or to determine how much time it requires?
qa_pairs.answer: To monitor the progress of your submitted job as it runs, or to find how much time your job needs, you should consider running an interactive job.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information about how job priority is determined and how time limits influence job scheduling?
qa_pairs.answer: For a discussion of how job priority is determined and how things like time limits may affect the scheduling of your jobs, please see [[Job scheduling policies]].

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if jobs within my research group are competing with each other?
qa_pairs.answer: If jobs within your research group are competing with one another, please see [[Managing_Slurm_accounts|Managing Slurm accounts]].

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find comprehensive documentation and tutorials for Slurm?
qa_pairs.answer: Comprehensive documentation is maintained by SchedMD at [https://slurm.schedmd.com/documentation.html], and some tutorials are available at [https://slurm.schedmd.com/tutorials.html].

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find detailed options for the `sbatch` command?
qa_pairs.answer: Detailed options for the `sbatch` command can be found on its online manual page: [https://slurm.schedmd.com/sbatch.html].

qa_pairs.question: Is there a resource that maps commands and directives from other job schedulers (like PBS/Torque, SGE, LSF, LoadLeveler) to Slurm?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a "Rosetta stone" available at [https://slurm.schedmd.com/rosetta.pdf] that maps commands and directives from PBS/Torque, SGE, LSF, and LoadLeveler to Slurm.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I access a text tutorial for Slurm from CÉCI, Belgium?
qa_pairs.answer: A text tutorial for Slurm from CÉCI, Belgium, is available at [http://www.ceci-hpc.be/slurm_tutorial.html].

qa_pairs.question: Can you recommend a minimal text tutorial for basic Slurm usage on Linux clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: You can find a minimal text tutorial from Bright Computing on basic Slurm usage for Linux clusters at [http://www.brightcomputing.com/blog/bid/174099/slurm-101-basic-slurm-usage-for-linux-clusters].
